title: Authors and Citation
text: Natheo Beauchamp. Author, maintainer.            R package developer Gauthier Ligot. Author.            Java Capsis developer algorithm improvement Francois de Coligny. Author.            Java Capsis developer Maxime Jaunatre. Author.            R package support developer Benoit Courbaud. Author, copyright holder.            Algorithm theory creator

title: Authors and Citation
text: Beauchamp N, Ligot G, de Coligny F, Jaunatre M, Courbaud B (2026). SamsaRaLight: R Package Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing. R package version 1.0.0, https://natheob.github.io/SamsaRaLight/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {SamsaRaLight: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing},   author = {Natheo Beauchamp and Gauthier Ligot and Francois {de Coligny} and Maxime Jaunatre and Benoit Courbaud},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://natheob.github.io/SamsaRaLight/}, }

what: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing
title: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing
text: SamsaRaLight R package simulating light transmission forest stands based ray-tracing model SamsaraLight (Courbaud et al. 2003). 📊 allows users estimate: Light intercepted individual trees Light reaching forest floor Light virtual ground sensors ✨ many features: Estimation direct diffuse radiation Flexible crown geometry (symmetric asymmetric) Multiple transmission models Light competition indicators Virtual light sensors 2D visualization tools Parallel simulations 🌐 Model documentation tutorials available : https://natheob.github.io/SamsaRaLight/

title: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing
text: can install development version SamsaRaLight GitHub :

title: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing
text: use SamsaraLight research, please cite : Courbaud, B., de Coligny, F. & Cordonnier, T. Simulating radiation distribution heterogeneous Norway spruce forest slope. Agricultural Forest Meteorology 116, 1–18 (2003). Courbaud et al. 2003. package: published soon obtain DOI cited research tool.

title: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing
text: Contributions, bug reports, feature requests welcome. Please open issue pull request github project.

title: An R Package to Estimate Tree Light Interception Using Ray-Tracing
text: package released GPL-3 License. See LICENSE details.

what: SamsaRaLight: Forest light interception and radiation simulation — SamsaRaLight-package
title: SamsaRaLight: Forest light interception and radiation simulation — SamsaRaLight-package
text: SamsaRaLight R package simulating forest stand light interception, energy balance radiation transfer using spatially explicit tree crowns, sensors grid cells.

title: SamsaRaLight: Forest light interception and radiation simulation — SamsaRaLight-package
text: package provides tools : Build forest stands trees, cells sensors Run ray-tracing based light simulations Compute tree, cell sensor energy variables Summarize visualize simulation outputs

title: SamsaRaLight: Forest light interception and radiation simulation — SamsaRaLight-package
text: Maintainer: Natheo Beauchamp beauchamp.natheo@gmail.com (ORCID) (R package developer) Authors: Gauthier Ligot gligot@uliege.(ORCID) (Java Capsis developer algorithm improvement) Francois de Coligny francois.decoligny@inrae.fr (ORCID) (Java Capsis developer) Maxime Jaunatre maxime.jaunatre@inrae.fr (ORCID) (R package support developer) Benoit Courbaud benoit.courbaud@inrae.fr (ORCID) (Algorithm theory creator) [copyright holder]

what: Example forest inventory datasets for SamsaRaLight — SamsaRaLight_data
title: Example forest inventory datasets for SamsaRaLight — SamsaRaLight_data
text: datasets provide example forest inventories used light interception simulations SamsaRaLight package. dataset named list 5 elements: trees, sensors, core_polygon, radiations, info.

title: Example forest inventory datasets for SamsaRaLight — SamsaRaLight_data
code: data(data_prenovel) data(data_IRRES1) data(data_bechefa) data(data_cloture20)  data_prenovel  data_IRRES1  data_bechefa  data_cloture20

title: Example forest inventory datasets for SamsaRaLight — SamsaRaLight_data
text: named list 5 elements: trees data.frame tree-level data: id_tree Unique id tree (integer). species Latin species name (character). x, y Coordinates base tree meters (numeric). dbh_cm Diameter breast height cm (numeric). crown_type Crown shape type, e.g. "P", "E", complex types like "8E" (character). h_m Height tree trunk meters (numeric). hbase_m Height crown base meters (numeric). hmax_m Height crown radius maximum, NA used (numeric). rn_m, rs_m, re_m, rw_m Crown radii meters (numeric). crown_lad Leaf area density crown (m2/m3) (numeric). sensors data.frame sensor data: id_sensor Unique sensor ID (integer). x, y Coordinates sensor meters (numeric). h_m Height sensor meters (numeric). pacl, pacl_direct, pacl_diffuse Proportion -canopy light measured sensor (numeric). May NULL sensors present. core_polygon data.frame vertices inventory polygon: x, y Coordinates polygon vertices meters (numeric). radiations data.frame monthly radiation: month Month number (integer). Hrad Monthly radiation MJ (numeric). DGratio Diffuse--global radiation ratio (numeric). info named list site information: latitude, longitude Coordinates site decimal degrees (numeric). slope Mean slope degrees (numeric). aspect Aspect degrees north (numeric). north2x Angle north x-axis clockwise degrees (numeric). object class list length 5. object class list length 5. object class list length 5. object class list length 5.

title: Example forest inventory datasets for SamsaRaLight — SamsaRaLight_data
text: Courbaud Benoit (INRAe LESSEM Grenoble) Gauthier Ligot (Gembloux Agro-Bio Tech) Gauthier Ligot (Gembloux Agro-Bio Tech) Gauthier Ligot (Gembloux Agro-Bio Tech)

what: Check coordinate columns and determine whether conversion is required — check_coordinates
title: Check coordinate columns and determine whether conversion is required — check_coordinates
text: function checks whether data frame contains valid planar coordinates (x, y) geographic coordinates (lon, lat). geographic coordinates detected, user informed must converted planar coordinate system using create_xy_from_lonlat().

title: Check coordinate columns and determine whether conversion is required — check_coordinates
Check coordinate columns and determine whether conversion is required — check_coordinates
text: df data.frame containing spatial coordinates. verbose Logical. TRUE, informative messages printed.

title: Check coordinate columns and determine whether conversion is required — check_coordinates
text: Logical. Invisibly returns TRUE coordinates must converted longitude/latitude planar coordinates, FALSE planar coordinates already present.

title: Check coordinate columns and determine whether conversion is required — check_coordinates
code: df_xy <- data.frame(x = 1:3, y = 4:6) check_coordinates(df_xy) #> Planar coordinates successfully validated.  df_lonlat <- data.frame(lon = c(10, 11), lat = c(45, 46)) check_coordinates(df_lonlat, verbose = TRUE) #> Geographic coordinates (`lon`, `lat`) detected.

what: Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
title: Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
text: function checks whether tree inventory data.frame correctly formatted used input ray-tracing model. verifies presence, type, validity mandatory optional variables describing geometry attributes trees within forest stand.

title: Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
text: trees_inv data.frame one row per tree following columns: id_tree Unique identifier tree (numeric character, duplicates). x X position tree within stand (numeric, meters, planar coordinates). Optional lon lat provided. y Y position tree within stand (numeric, meters, planar coordinates). Optional lon lat provided. lon Longitude tree location (numeric, decimal degrees). Optional; provided lat, coordinates converted internally UTM planar coordinate system create_sl_stand(). lat Latitude tree location (numeric, decimal degrees). Optional; see lon. species Species name (character). dbh_cm Diameter breast height (1.3 m, cm). crown_type Type crown geometry. One "E", "P", "2E", "8E", "4P". h_m Total tree height (numeric, meters). hbase_m Height crown base (numeric, meters). hmax_m Height maximum crown radius (numeric, meters). Required least one tree crown type "2E" "8E". crown types, column optional value internally computed. rn_m Crown radius toward North (numeric, meters). rs_m Crown radius toward South (numeric, meters). re_m Crown radius toward East (numeric, meters). rw_m Crown radius toward West (numeric, meters). crown_lad Leaf Area Density (m2/m3). crown_openness Crown openness (unitless), optional turbid medium interception. Required argument turbid_medium = FALSE advanced function run_sl_advanced() (porous envelope interception). Otherwise, basic function run_sl() automatically computed interception turbid medium using crown_lad variable. verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages warnings printed.

title: Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
text: Invisibly returns TRUE checks pass.

title: Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
text: function performs following checks validations: 1 Ensures trees_inv non-empty data.frame required columns. 2 Checks id_tree values unique. 3 Checks either planar coordinates (x, y) geographic coordinates (lon, lat) provided. lon lat supplied, converted planar UTM coordinates create_sl_stand(). plot_inventory() function requires planar coordinates used conversion. 4 Validates numeric columns (dbh_cm, h_m, hbase_m, rn_m, rs_m, re_m, rw_m) numeric non-negative. 5 Verifies crown_type values one "E", "P", "2E", "8E", "4P". 6 Ensures crown radii present according crown type. 7 Checks hmax_m provided required lies hbase_m h_m. 8 Ensures hbase_m < h_m. 9 Verifies tree crown LAD defined. 10 Provides informative error messages warnings invalid conditions.

title: Check the format and validity of a tree inventory data.frame — check_inventory
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Using the example dataset from the package data_prenovel <- SamsaRaLight::data_prenovel trees <- data_prenovel$trees  # Check the inventory check_inventory(trees)  # Quiet mode check_inventory(trees, verbose = FALSE) } # }

title: Validate monthly radiation input — check_monthly_radiations
text: Checks monthly radiation data.frame correctly formatted physically valid use light interception ray-tracing model. table must contain exactly 12 months radiation data.

title: Validate monthly radiation input — check_monthly_radiations
Validate monthly radiation input — check_monthly_radiations
text: x data.frame monthly radiation values, typically produced get_monthly_radiations. verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages printed.

title: Validate monthly radiation input — check_monthly_radiations
text: Invisibly returns TRUE checks pass.

title: Validate monthly radiation input — check_monthly_radiations
text: input must contain following columns: month Integer month number (1-12) Hrad Monthly global horizontal irradiation (MJ/m2) DGratio Diffuse--global radiation ratio (unitless, 0-1) function checks: 1 object data.frame. 2 Required columns present. 3 exactly 12 months. 4 month (1-12) present exactly . 5 Data numeric finite. 6 Hrad strictly positive. 7 DGratio 0 1. 8 Months increasing order.

what: Check and validate a polygon defined by vertices — check_polygon
title: Check and validate a polygon defined by vertices — check_polygon
text: function converts data.frame polygon vertices sf POLYGON checks validity. polygon invalid, attempts fix .

title: Check and validate a polygon defined by vertices — check_polygon
code: check_polygon(core_polygon_df, trees_inv, sensors = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Check and validate a polygon defined by vertices — check_polygon
text: core_polygon_df data.frame columns x y defining polygon vertices trees_inv data.frame one row per tree. See check_inventory required structure validated columns. sensors Optional data.frame defining position height sensor within stand. See check_sensors required structure validated columns. verbose Logical. TRUE, warnings printed

title: Check and validate a polygon defined by vertices — check_polygon
text: data.frame polygon vertices (x, y): unchanged valid modified fixed (warning)

what: Check the format and validity of a sensor position data.frame — check_sensors
title: Check the format and validity of a sensor position data.frame — check_sensors
text: function checks whether sensor data.frame correctly formatted used input ray-tracing model. verifies presence, type, validity mandatory variables describing position height sensors within forest stand.

title: Check the format and validity of a sensor position data.frame — check_sensors
Check the format and validity of a sensor position data.frame — check_sensors
text: sensors data.frame one row per sensor following columns: id_sensor Unique identifier sensor (numeric character, duplicates) x X position sensor (numeric, meters) y Y position sensor (numeric, meters) h_m Height ground sensor (numeric, meters) verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages printed.

title: Check the format and validity of a sensor position data.frame — check_sensors
text: Invisibly returns TRUE checks pass.

title: Check the format and validity of a sensor position data.frame — check_sensors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sensors <- data.frame(   id_sensor = 1:3,   x = c(10, 20, 30),   y = c(5, 15, 25),   h_m = c(1.5, 2.0, 1.8) ) check_sensors(sensors) } # }

what: Convert data.frame from lon/lat to UTM planar coordinates — convert_lonlat
title: Convert data.frame from lon/lat to UTM planar coordinates — convert_lonlat
text: Convert data.frame lon/lat UTM planar coordinates

title: Convert data.frame from lon/lat to UTM planar coordinates — convert_lonlat
Convert data.frame from lon/lat to UTM planar coordinates — convert_lonlat
text: df data.frame containing lon lat columns epsg_utm EPSG code UTM projection

title: Convert data.frame from lon/lat to UTM planar coordinates — convert_lonlat

what: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: function builds virtual forest stand user-provided tree inventory. inventory zone (core polygon) can optionally modified (e.g., replaced enclosing rectangle axis-aligned rectangle) constructing stand. Trees spatially shifted resulting inventory zone centered within regular grid square cells. Optionally, additional trees can added around core inventory area match basal area per hectare.

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
code: create_sl_stand(   trees_inv,   cell_size,   latitude,   slope,   aspect,   north2x,   sensors = NULL,   core_polygon_df = NULL,   modify_polygon = c("none", "rect", "aarect"),   fill_around = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: trees_inv data.frame one row per tree. See check_inventory required structure validated columns. cell_size Numeric. Side length square cells composing stand (meters). latitude Numeric. Latitude stand (degrees). slope Numeric. Slope plot (degrees). aspect Numeric. Aspect slope, defined azimuth downslope direction, clockwise North (degrees). (0 degrees: North-facing slope, 90 degrees: East-facing slope, 180 degrees: South-facing slope, 270 degrees: West-facing slope) north2x Numeric. Clockwise angle North X-axis (degrees). default 90 degrees corresponds Y-axis oriented toward true North (0 degrees: x-axis points North, 90 degrees: x-axis points East, 180 degrees: x-axis points South, 270 degrees: x-axis points West). sensors Optional data.frame defining position height sensor within stand. See check_sensors required structure validated columns. core_polygon_df Optional data.frame defining core inventory polygon. Must contain columns x y. NULL, concave hull automatically computed tree positions. modify_polygon Character. Defines inventory polygon modified. One : "none": core polygon used provided computed. "rect": polygon replaced minimum enclosing rectangle. "aarect": polygon replaced axis-aligned enclosing rectangle. fill_around Logical. TRUE, trees added outside core polygon basal area per hectare full stand matches core inventory. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), messages warnings printed processing. FALSE, output silent.

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: named list following elements: trees Data.frame final tree inventory, including added trees fill_around = TRUE. structure matches inventory format validated check_inventory, additional derived variables required ray tracing. logical column added_to_fill indicates whether tree originates initial inventory added fill around inventory zone. core_polygon List describing inventory zone: df: data.frame polygon vertices sf: corresponding sf POLYGON modify_polygon: applied polygon modification transform List transformation filling information, including core area, target final basal area, number added trees, applied spatial transformations. geometry List describing stand geometry terrain parameters (cell size, number cells, slope, aspect, orientation).

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: function supports sloping terrain coordinate system rotation, returns fully prepared stand ready use ray-tracing pipeline (see run_sl). returned trees data.frame conforms inventory format checked check_inventory, following controlled modifications: Tree vertical position z computed terrain slope aspect. Crown maximum radius height hmax_m computed fixed crown geometry conventions: "P" "4P": hmax_m = hbase_m "E" "4E": hmax_m = hbase_m + 0.5 * (h_m - hbase_m) crown types "2E" "8E", hmax_m must provided user. missing, column dbh_cm added filled NA. missing, crown interception properties (e.g. crown_openness, crown_lad) added using default values. input data.frames (trees_inv, sensors, core_polygon_df) automatically checked coordinate type: x y columns exist, assumed planar. lon lat columns exist, converted planar UTM coordinates automatically using create_xy_from_lonlat(). UTM projection (EPSG) determined mean coordinates trees_inv. inputs must share EPSG; otherwise, function stops error. conversion occurred, EPSG stored output. function ensures trees fall within core inventory polygon, applying small buffers necessary handle numerical precision issues. Invalid polygons automatically repaired possible. fill_around = TRUE, trees randomly sampled original inventory positioned outside core polygon target basal area per hectare reached full stand.

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data_prenovel <- SamsaRaLight::data_prenovel trees_inv <- data_prenovel$trees  stand <- create_sl_stand(   trees_inv = trees_inv,   cell_size = 5,   latitude = 46,   slope = 10,   aspect = 180,   north2x = 0,   modify_polygon = "aarect",   fill_around = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )  head(stand$trees) } # }

title: Plot a SamsaRaLight output — plot.sl_output

code: # S3 method for class 'sl_output' plot(   x,   ...,   what_trees = c("compet", "intercepted", "potential"),   what_cells = c("relative", "absolute"),   show_trees = TRUE,   direct_energy = NULL )

title: Plot a SamsaRaLight output — plot.sl_output
text: x object class sl_output, returned run_sl(). ... Additional arguments passed lower-level plotting functions. what_trees Character; tree metric plot. Choices : "compet" Light competition index (LCI), reversed viridis scale. "intercepted" Intercepted energy (MJ). "potential" Potential intercepted energy (MJ). Default "compet". what_cells Character; cell (ground) metric plot. Choices : "relative" Proportion canopy light (PACL)). "absolute" Energy ground (MJ/m2). Default "relative". show_trees Logical; whether display trees top ground light map. Default TRUE. direct_energy Logical NULL. NULL (default), total radiation outputs plotted (direct + diffuse). TRUE, direct radiation components plotted. FALSE, diffuse radiation components plotted. option requires detailed_output = TRUE running simulation

text: data.frame rotated coordinates: x Rotated x coordinates y Rotated y coordinates

title: Rotate 2D vectors by an angle (counter-clockwise) — rotate_vec_ccw
text: rotation follows: $$ x' = x \cos(\theta) - y \sin(\theta) $$ $$ y' = x \sin(\theta) + y \cos(\theta) $$

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
text: function computes light interception radiative balance forest stand using SamsaRaLight ray-tracing engine.

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
code: run_sl(   sl_stand,   monthly_radiations,   sensors_only = FALSE,   detailed_output = FALSE,   parallel_mode = FALSE,   n_threads = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
text: sl_stand object class "sl_stand" describing forest stand, created create_sl_stand. contains trees, sensors, terrain, grid geometry. monthly_radiations data.frame monthly horizontal radiation (Hrad, MJ m\(^{-2}\)) diffuse--global ratio (DGratio), typically obtained using get_monthly_radiations checked using check_monthly_radiations.. sensors_only Logical. TRUE, compute light interception sensors (much faster). detailed_output Logical. TRUE, output contains detailed diffuse/direct energies light datasets, full interception matrices interceptions output ray discretization monthy_rays. FALSE, total energies returned (recommended uses). parallel_mode logical. TRUE, ray–target computations parallelised using OpenMP. FALSE, model runs single-thread mode. SamsaRaLight uses OpenMP ray–target parallelisation. avoid competition OpenMP BLAS (matrix algebra libraries), BLAS automatically forced single-thread mode simulation. Using parallel_mode = TRUE strongly recommended large stands fine ray discretisation, computation time scales almost linearly number available CPU cores. n_threads integer NULL. Number CPU threads use parallel_mode = TRUE. NULL (default), OpenMP automatically selects number available cores. provided, must positive integer. verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages printed.

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
text: object class "sl_output", containing: light list data.frames simulated light interception: trees: light intercepted trees cells: light received ground cells sensors: light received sensors info list metadata simulation (latitude, sky type, torus use, etc.). monthly_rays (detailed_output = TRUE) Discretization monthly radiations interceptions (detailed_output = TRUE) interception matrices trees rays cell/sensor

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
text: standard user interface SamsaRaLight. Advanced ray-tracing sky discretization parameters internally set robust defaults need provided. Internally, run_sl() calls advanced engine run_sl_advanced() fixed ray-tracing sky discretization. normally use SamsaRaLight:::run_sl_advanced() directly unless developing new ray-tracing configurations methodological work.

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data_prenovel <- SamsaRaLight::data_prenovel  stand <- create_sl_stand(   trees = data_prenovel$trees,   sensors = data_prenovel$sensors,   cell_size = 5,   latitude = 45.8,   slope = 10,   aspect = 180,   north2x = 0 )  rad <- get_monthly_radiations(45.8, 3.1)  out <- run_sl(   sl_stand = stand,   monthly_radiations = rad )  out$light$trees } # }

what: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
text: function runs full light interception radiative balance simulation virtual forest stand advanced parameters. allows customization ray discretization, sky type trunk interception.

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
code: run_sl_advanced(   sl_stand,   monthly_radiations,   sensors_only = FALSE,   use_torus = TRUE,   turbid_medium = TRUE,   extinction_coef = 0.5,   clumping_factor = 1,   trunk_interception = TRUE,   height_anglemin = 10,   direct_startoffset = 0,   direct_anglestep = 5,   diffuse_anglestep = 15,   soc = TRUE,   start_day = 1,   end_day = 365,   detailed_output = FALSE,   parallel_mode = FALSE,   n_threads = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
text: sl_stand object class "sl_stand" representing virtual stand. row tree required optional columns describing crown geometry, height, crown radius, crown openness, LAD, etc. See validate_sl_stand. monthly_radiations data.frame monthly horizontal radiation (Hrad) diffuse global ratio (DGratio), computed get_monthly_radiations. sensors_only logical, TRUE, compute interception sensors use_torus logical, TRUE, use torus system borders turbid_medium logical, TRUE, crowns considered turbid medium (using column crown_lad), else porous envelope (using column crown_openess) extinction_coef Numeric scalar. Leaf extinction coefficient controlling probability ray intercepted foliage. represents effective light attenuation per unit leaf area linked average leaf orientation. Higher values increase interception (default = 0.5). clumping_factor Numeric scalar controlling aggregation leaves within crown volume. value 1 corresponds homogeneous (random) foliage distribution; values < 1 indicate clumped foliage, values > 1 indicate regular spacing. modifies effective light interception turbid medium model (default = 1). trunk_interception logical, TRUE, account trunk interception height_anglemin numeric, minimum altitude angle rays (degrees) direct_startoffset numeric, starting angle first direct ray (degrees) direct_anglestep numeric, hour angle step direct rays (degrees) diffuse_anglestep numeric, hour angle step diffuse rays (degrees) soc logical, TRUE, use Standard Overcast Sky; FALSE, Uniform Overcast Sky start_day integer, first day vegetative period (1–365) end_day integer, last day vegetative period (1–365) detailed_output logical, TRUE, include detailed rays, energies, interception matrices parallel_mode logical. TRUE, ray–target computations parallelised using OpenMP. FALSE, model runs single-thread mode. n_threads integer NULL. Number CPU threads use parallel_mode = TRUE. NULL (default), OpenMP automatically selects number available cores. provided, must positive integer. verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages printed.

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
text: object class "sl_output" (list) containing: light: list simulation outputs trees, cells, sensors info: list run metadata (latitude, days, sky type, etc.) monthly_rays (detailed_output = TRUE): ray discretization per month interceptions (detailed_output = TRUE): tree/cell interception matrices

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
text: typical use, see simpler run_sl wrapper sets standard discretization parameters users. advanced function exposes ray tracing parameters intended users need full control ray discretization modeling options. users, see run_sl wraps function default parameters suitable standard runs.

title: Validate a SamsaRaLight simulation output object — validate_sl_output
text: function called internally end simulation ensure returned object valid complete provided user. function checks : object inherits class "sl_output". Top-level components $output $input exist. $output$light contains trees, cells, sensors. elements data.frames. Required identifier energy columns present. Energy-related columns numeric. detailed outputs requested, presence structure $output$monthly_rays $output$interceptions also verified available.

what: Validate a SamsaRaLight stand object — validate_sl_stand
title: Validate a SamsaRaLight stand object — validate_sl_stand
text: function checks internal consistency structure object class "sl_stand", returned create_sl_stand. verifies required components present correctly formatted, embedded tree inventory conforms rules enforced check_inventory, trees sensors within stand limits.

text: x object expected class "sl_stand".

title: Validate a SamsaRaLight stand object — validate_sl_stand
text: Invisibly returns TRUE stand valid.

title: Validate a SamsaRaLight stand object — validate_sl_stand
text: following validations performed: object inherits class "sl_stand". top-level components trees, sensors, cells, core_polygon, transform, geometry inventory present. trees data.frame passes check_inventory. sensors data.frame passes check_sensors. cells data.frame contains columns x_center, y_center, z_center, id_cell. geometry list contains cell_size, n_cells_x, n_cells_y, slope, aspect, north2x. trees sensors lie within bounds rectangular stand.

title: Validate a SamsaRaLight stand object — validate_sl_stand
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data_prenovel <- SamsaRaLight::data_prenovel stand <- create_sl_stand(data_prenovel$trees, cell_size = 5,                          slope = 10, aspect = 180, north2x = 0)  validate_sl_stand(stand)  # returns TRUE invisibly } # }
